ops.body: Root cause: go-git v5.19.1 filesystem.ObjectStorage seeds its packfile index on the first pack-touching read (requireIndex, object.go:53) and never rescans objects/pack. Sync opens one gitobj.Repo across up to 5 fetch rounds; an exec'd git fetch delivering >= transfer.unpackLimit (default 100) objects lands them in a NEW pack invisible to the seeded index, so the final downloadAttachments ReadChain got ErrObjectNotFound -> 'incomplete chain ... (shallow clone?)' while git cat-file (fresh process) saw the object. Loose objects are never stale in our non-ExclusiveAccess config, which is why small fetches never triggered it. Upstream: go-git#1623 (closed not-planned), go-git#2242 (v5.19.1, open); real git's odb.c rescans packs and retries once on a miss — the remedy go-git lacks. Fixed in b93175ae by wrapping every gitobj object read in reindex-and-retry (ErrObjectNotFound + ErrPackfileNotFound), with shallow-aware wording: authoritative misses say 'missing from object database'; the shallow hint survives only when .git/shallow exists.
